failed_assertions: Contains ## Overview section with project description
Contains ## Overview section with project description
Contains a commands or build section with make targets
Listed make targets match actual Makefile targets
Uses Pointer Principle (references files, doesn't duplicate content)
Structure is concise (under 100 lines for a simple skill repo)
Structure is concise (under 100 lines for a simple skill repo)

qualitative_notes.structure: with_skill produces consistent AGENTS-GENERATED sections (commands, filemap, golden-samples, utilities, heuristics, repo-settings, codebase-state, scope-index). without_skill varies widely: sparse 57-line output for Go project vs bloated 466-line output for TYPO3.
qualitative_notes.pointer_principle: with_skill consistently references files without duplicating code. without_skill TYPO3 output includes ~200 lines of code examples that belong in documentation.
qualitative_notes.actionability: with_skill outputs include heuristics tables, golden samples, and utilities -- enabling agents to make quick decisions. without_skill outputs describe architecture but lack decision-support structures.
qualitative_notes.consistency: with_skill outputs follow a uniform template across all 3 project types. without_skill outputs have different structures for each project type.
qualitative_notes.accuracy: Both variants achieve high accuracy for stated facts. with_skill verifies more paths and commands, providing more opportunities for correctness (or error).
qualitative_notes.biggest_win: Go project eval shows the largest delta (+38%). without_skill produced only 57 lines missing most Makefile targets and all structured tables.
qualitative_notes.shared_weakness: Neither variant includes a ## Overview section with a project description paragraph (Go project). Both exceed 100-line limit for skill repo (though the repo is complex enough to justify it).
